_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to analyse and evaluate claims for accuracy, precision, depth and breadth, and to identify when claims are used in misleading ways.

_source.w: We learn to analyse and evaluate claims for accuracy, precision, depth and breadth, and to identify when claims are used in misleading ways.
y: Evaluating the quality of claims is essential for navigating a world of competing information.
Recognising when claims are used misleadingly protects us from manipulation.
These critical evaluation skills are fundamental to informed citizenship and intellectual integrity.
eg: We evaluate claims for accuracy — are they factually correct and well-supported?
We assess precision — are the claims specific enough to be meaningful and testable?
We analyse depth — do the claims address the full complexity of the issue?
We evaluate breadth — do the claims consider relevant perspectives and evidence?
We identify misleading uses of claims — cherry-picking, misrepresentation, out-of-context quotation.
We analyse real-world examples of misleading claims in media, advertising and political discourse.
